_meta.description: Codebook for the NASN cleaned table CSV files produced by the OCR pipeline (TableCleaner.py + StationResolver.py). Documents every column in both the 16-column format (Table 1) and 15-column format (Tables 2-31).

table_formats.16_column: Table 1 (Suspended Particulate Matter) - includes Site column

Station_Location.description: Raw OCR-extracted station name string as it appeared in the scanned document. May contain OCR errors such as character substitutions, extra spaces, or abbreviated state names (e.g. 'WATERBURY CONN', 'NEW YORK N Y'). Use Resolved_Name for analysis; keep this column for audit and QA.
Station_Location.ocr_notes: Common patterns: city name in all caps followed by abbreviated state. OCR errors include: character substitution (0 for O, 1 for l), merged words, extra punctuation.
Station_Location.forward_fill_note: Station name appears only on the first row for a given station. Subsequent year rows for the same station have the name forward-filled from the first row.

Site.description: Sampling site number within a city. Present only in Table 1 (Suspended Particulates) because multiple sites per city were operated in 1953-1956. After January 1, 1957 only one site per city was maintained.
Site.valid_values: Integer 1 through 9. Blank for rows where site number was not captured by OCR.

site_meaning.context: Sites were located in commercial, industrial, residential, and rural sections of urban or suburban areas to sample distinctly different environments within the same community.
site_meaning.note: Do not average across sites for a city without understanding what each site represents. Site 1 is typically the primary commercial or downtown site.

Site.aggregate_note: Rows with Is_Site_Aggregate=True summarise across multiple sites (e.g. all sites for a city for a given year).

Years.description: Two-digit calendar year code(s) for the samples in this row. Single year = individual annual record. Multiple years separated by space = aggregate row summarising those combined years.
Years.analysis_note: For independent observations use only rows where Is_Year_Aggregate=False. For overall station summaries use the aggregate rows, but never mix them with individual year rows in the same statistical calculation.
Years.sampling_note: 1953-1956 used weekly sampling (approx 26-52 samples per year). 1957 used biweekly randomized sampling (approx 26 samples per year). Num_Samples reflects this.

Num_Samples.description: Number of individual 24-hour filter samples collected during the year(s) shown in the Years column.

expected_ranges.weekly_1953_to_1956: Approximately 26-52 per year depending on how much of the year the station operated

Num_Samples.missing_values: NaN indicates OCR could not reliably read the value. Verify against source PDF using Page_Num.
Num_Samples.validation: If Num_Samples is unusually high (e.g. >200 for a single year) the row may be a multi-year aggregate that Is_Year_Aggregate did not flag, or an OCR error.

Min.description: Minimum observed concentration across all individual samples collected during the year(s) shown.
Min.units: See table-level units in historical_context.json. Tables 1-3: ug/m3. Tables 5-31: ug/m3 x 10^-3. Table 4: uuCi/m3 x 10^-3.
Min.validation: Min <= Avg should hold. Rows violating this have Min set to NaN by TableCleaner.
Min.missing_values: NaN if OCR could not read the value.

Max.description: Maximum observed concentration across all individual samples collected during the year(s) shown.
Max.units: Same as Min. See historical_context.json tables section.
Max.validation: Max >= Avg should hold. Rows violating this have Max set to NaN by TableCleaner.
Max.interpretation: Very high Max relative to Avg (high Max/Avg ratio) suggests episodic pollution events such as temperature inversions, industrial incidents, or seasonal burning.
Max.missing_values: NaN if OCR could not read the value.

Avg.description: Arithmetic mean concentration across all individual samples collected during the year(s) shown.
Avg.units: Same as Min. See historical_context.json tables section.
Avg.statistical_warning: The original publication (page 245) confirms all frequency distributions are log-normal. For log-normal data the arithmetic mean is inflated by high outliers and overestimates the typical concentration. The geometric mean (approximated by P50/median) is the more appropriate measure of central tendency for most analyses.
Avg.validation: Min <= Avg <= Max should hold. TableCleaner clears violating values.
Avg.missing_values: NaN if OCR could not read the value.

P10.description: 10th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution of all individual 24-hour sample concentrations collected during the year(s) shown. 10% of samples had concentrations at or below this value.
P10.missing_values: NaN if OCR could not read the value or if value was removed due to percentile ordering violation.

P20.description: 20th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution.
P20.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation.

P30.description: 30th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution.
P30.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation.

P40.description: 40th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution.
P40.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation.

P50.description: 50th percentile (median) of the cumulative frequency distribution. For a log-normal distribution this closely approximates the geometric mean and is the most appropriate single summary statistic.
P50.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation.

P60.description: 60th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution.
P60.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation.

P70.description: 70th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution.
P70.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation.

P80.description: 80th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution.
P80.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation.

P90.description: 90th percentile of the cumulative frequency distribution. 90% of samples had concentrations at or below this value. A high P90/P50 ratio indicates a right-skewed distribution with frequent high-concentration episodes.
P90.missing_values: NaN if unreadable or ordering violation. P90 is near the right edge of the printed page and is one of the more commonly missing values due to OCR edge-clipping.
P90.percentile_ordering_note: TableCleaner enforces P10 <= P20 <= P30 <= P40 <= P50 <= P60 <= P70 <= P80 <= P90. Any value that violates this ordering is set to NaN.

Avg_Confidence.description: Mean Tesseract OCR word-level confidence score for all text tokens extracted from this row. Higher values indicate more reliable OCR extraction.

interpretation.60_to_80: Medium confidence. Worth spot-checking key values against source PDF.
interpretation.below_60: Lower confidence. Recommend manual verification for critical analyses.
interpretation.note: Confidence reflects the OCR engine certainty, not the accuracy of the data value itself. A high-confidence incorrect character is still wrong. Use in combination with logical validation (min<=avg<=max, etc.).

Page_Num.description: The page number within the reconstructed table pages (not the original PDF page number). Use this to locate the source row in the intermediate reconstruction outputs for manual verification.
Page_Num.note: To verify a value against the original scanned document, use this number to find the corresponding page image in the output/images/ directory.

Resolved_Name.description: Canonical station name resolved by StationResolver.py from the raw Station_Location OCR string. This is the primary identifier to use for grouping, joining, and analysis. Matches the canonical_name field in master_stations.csv.
Resolved_Name.matching_logic: Uses Python difflib SequenceMatcher with normalization (lowercase, punctuation stripped). Tries exact match first, then fuzzy match with bonuses for state and site number agreement.
Resolved_Name.fallback: If no match is found above the LOW_THRESHOLD (60.0), Resolved_Name equals the original Station_Location string (unresolved).
Resolved_Name.state_aggregate_rows: For state aggregate rows (e.g. 'Connecticut Total'), Resolved_Name equals the original Station_Location string since these do not map to any individual station.
Resolved_Name.join_key_note: Use Station_Order (not Resolved_Name) as the join key when combining data across different pollutant tables, because OCR errors may cause the same physical station to have slightly different Resolved_Name values across tables.

Station_Order.description: Sequential row number from the master station list (master_stations.csv). This number is the same for every pollutant table in the publication. It is the correct and reliable key for joining data across different pollutant tables.
Station_Order.source: Derived from Table D (Sampling Site Descriptions) of the original publication via master_stations.csv.
Station_Order.ordering: Urban stations 1-N (grouped New England → Pacific Coast), then Suburban, then Nonurban.
Station_Order.missing_values: NaN for rows that could not be matched to the master list (unmatched, state_aggregate, skip, low_confidence below threshold).
Station_Order.cross_table_joins: To combine suspended particulates (Table 1) with lead concentrations (Table 15) for the same stations, join on Station_Order: df_t1.merge(df_t15, on='Station_Order', suffixes=('_pm','_pb'))
Station_Order.asterisk_rule: Stations with Station_Order values corresponding to ** notes in the master list only appear in Tables 1-4. Those with * appear only in Tables 1-8. All others appear in all 31 tables.

Match_Score.description: Similarity score (0-100) between the raw Station_Location string and the matched canonical station name, calculated using Python difflib SequenceMatcher after normalization.

thresholds.100.0: Exact match after normalization (punctuation stripped, lowercase)
thresholds.60.0_to_84.9: Low confidence match. Accepted but flagged as low_confidence.
thresholds.below_60.0: No match found. Resolved_Name = original OCR string. Match_Type = unmatched.
thresholds.null: State aggregate rows, headers, or other non-station rows

Match_Type.description: Classification of the station name resolution result.

valid_values.exact: Match score = 100.0. Station name matched perfectly after normalization.
valid_values.high_confidence: Match score 85.0-99.9. Very likely correct. Auto-accepted.
valid_values.low_confidence: Match score 60.0-84.9. Plausible match but worth verifying for critical analyses.
valid_values.unmatched: Match score below 60.0 or no candidate found. Resolved_Name = original OCR string. Station_Order = NaN.
valid_values.state_aggregate: Row is a state-level total (e.g. 'Connecticut Total'). Present in 15-column tables only. Legitimate data row but does not map to any individual station.
valid_values.skip: Row is a table header, section header, or clearly non-station text. No resolution attempted.

Match_Type.analysis_filter: For analysis of individual monitoring station data: filter Match_Type IN ('exact', 'high_confidence'). Include 'low_confidence' with caution. Exclude 'unmatched', 'state_aggregate', 'skip'.

Is_Year_Aggregate.description: True if the Years column contains multiple year codes (e.g. '54 56', '53 57'), indicating this row summarises data across multiple calendar years combined.
Is_Year_Aggregate.critical_analysis_note: NEVER include Is_Year_Aggregate=True rows in the same statistical calculation as Is_Year_Aggregate=False rows for the same station. The data would be double-counted. Use False rows for time-series analysis and trend detection. True rows are useful only for overall station summaries when individual years are not needed.
Is_Year_Aggregate.typical_pattern: A station with data in years 1954, 1955, and 1956 will have three individual rows (Is_Year_Aggregate=False) plus one aggregate row with Years='54 55 56' (Is_Year_Aggregate=True).

Is_Site_Aggregate.description: True if this row aggregates data across multiple sampling sites within the same city. Present in Table 1 only (Suspended Particulates) where multiple sites per city were operated in 1953-1956.
Is_Site_Aggregate.critical_analysis_note: Like Is_Year_Aggregate, never mix Is_Site_Aggregate=True with individual site rows in the same statistical calculation for the same station-year. The data is double-counted.
Is_Site_Aggregate.typical_pattern: A city with 3 sites will have individual rows for Site=1, Site=2, Site=3, plus an aggregate row with Is_Site_Aggregate=True covering all three sites combined.

master_stations_columns.description: Columns in master_stations.csv, which is the reference file used by StationResolver.py

columns.station_order: Sequential integer. The universal join key across all 31 data tables.
columns.canonical_name: Authoritative city/station name. Used as Resolved_Name in cleaned outputs.
columns.region: NASN geographic region (New England, Mid Atlantic, etc.).
columns.site_num: Site number for multi-site cities (integer or blank).
columns.max_table: Highest table number this station appears in. 31 = all tables. 8 = Tables 1-8 only (single asterisk). 4 = Tables 1-4 only (double asterisk).
columns.display_name: Formatted display name as it appears in the original publication (e.g. 'Waterbury, Conn.').

recommended_filters.individual_station_year_analysis: Is_Year_Aggregate == False AND Is_Site_Aggregate == False AND Match_Type IN ('exact', 'high_confidence')
recommended_filters.include_low_confidence: Is_Year_Aggregate == False AND Is_Site_Aggregate == False AND Match_Type IN ('exact', 'high_confidence', 'low_confidence')
recommended_filters.urban_stations_only: Add: urban_type == 'Urban' (requires joining with master_stations.csv on Station_Order)

r_loading_example.note: Example R code to load a cleaned table with the codebook
code: # Individual year observations only, high-confidence station matches
# P50 (median) as better central tendency for log-normal data

python_loading_example.note: Example Python code to load a cleaned table with the codebook
